# shaolin

> A standalone modular CQRS/Event-Sourcing backend framework for Ruby (4.0+). Each entity is a
> self-contained folder (`app/modules/<name>/`) with an enforced public contract. Commands mutate
> event-sourced aggregates; events persist to a Postgres event store; projections build read
> models you query. HTTP and RabbitMQ are interchangeable transports over the same buses; async
> side effects run via a transactional outbox + worker. Designed to
> be operated by AI agents: deterministic conventions, generators, explicit requires, machine-
> readable contracts.

## Build an app

- **One gem, from git.** The umbrella `shaolin` gem pulls the whole stack. Distributed via private
  **git** (not RubyGems), so an app's `Gemfile` is one line —
  `gem "shaolin", git: "git@github.com:mantissa-lab/shaolin.git", glob: "gems/*/*.gemspec"` (the
  `glob:` lets Bundler see every gemspec in the repo, so the umbrella resolves all sub-gems) — and
  `config/boot.rb` is a single `require "shaolin"` (it loads core, cqrs, activerecord, dto, http,
  server, jobs, messaging, redis, rabbitmq, llm, harness; the `shaolin` CLI ships with it). Local
  framework dev via `shaolin new --path` uses the same one line with `path:` instead of `git:`.
- `shaolin new <app>`: scaffold a runnable app (Gemfile, config/boot.rb, bin/server, Dockerfile,
  Knative deploy/service.yaml, AGENTS.md).
- `shaolin g module <plural>`: scaffold a module that boots immediately. **Default is a plain CRUD
  module** (ActiveRecord model + DTO + controller + migration). Add **`--es`** for an event-sourced
  CQRS module (command/event/aggregate/projection/read-model — pay the ceremony only when you need
  event sourcing). Inflection: `orders` → namespace `Orders`, entity `Order`; with `--es` also command
  `CreateOrder`, event `OrderCreated`, read model `orders_read`, topic `orders.order_created`.
- `shaolin server` (Falcon), `shaolin console`, `shaolin migrate`, `shaolin routes`.
- **Agent tools:** `shaolin describe --json` (machine-readable map of all modules: commands,
  events, imports, exports — load this to understand the whole app), `shaolin schemas`
  (command/event surface), `shaolin lint` (isolation check — flags cross-module reach-ins, including
  in reactors; also warns on app code OUTSIDE app/modules that reads `Shaolin::Kernel[...]` or another
  module's constants — `--strict`/`SHAOLIN_LINT_STRICT=1` fails on those), `shaolin graph` (module
  dependency graph), `shaolin projections rebuild [name]`
  (replay events into read models), `shaolin g module <name> --es` (event-sourced CQRS module; default
  is CRUD), `shaolin g module <name> --es --reactor` (also scaffold an async reactor + spec),
  `shaolin g field <module> <name>:<type>` (add-column migration + edit checklist),
  `shaolin db reset` (dev: drop+create+migrate; lint also flags undeclared `import`s),
  `shaolin openapi [--out FILE]` (OpenAPI 3.1 from controllers' routes + DTO request schemas; or serve it
  live + Swagger UI via `HTTP.register_provider!(swagger: true)` → `GET /swagger` + `/openapi.json`, on
  in dev by default).
- **Async runtimes:** `shaolin worker` (process the outbox — runs reactors; `WORKER_CONCURRENCY=N`
  threads, retries with exponential backoff, dead-letter on exhaustion, graceful SIGTERM),
  `shaolin scheduler` (fire `Shaolin.schedule` tasks; single leader via a Postgres advisory lock).
  `describe --json` also lists each module's `reactors` (with subscribed events) and top-level
  `scheduled` tasks.

## Module anatomy (`app/modules/<name>/`)

- `module.rb` — manifest: `Shaolin.module "<name>" do imports/exports/commands_handled/events_published end`. The public boundary.
- `commands/<verb>_<entity>.rb` — `Shaolin::ValueObject` (typed intent).
- `events/<entity>_<pasttense>.rb` — `RubyEventStore::Event` subclass (domain event).
- `<entity>.rb` — aggregate: `include Shaolin::CQRS::Aggregate`; command methods call `apply(Event.new(data:))`; `on(Event) { |e| ... }` mutates replay state.
- `command_handlers/<...>_handler.rb` — `< Shaolin::CQRS::CommandHandler`; `handles SomeCommand`; `def call(cmd) ... end`; use `aggregate_repository.unit_of_work(Aggregate.new(id)) { |a| a.do }`; return `Dry::Monads Success/Failure`.
- `projections/<name>_projection.rb` — `< Shaolin::CQRS::Projection`; `on(Event) { |e| ReadModels::X.project(id: e.data[:id]) { |r| ... } }`.
- `read_models/<entity>_record.rb` — `< Shaolin::AR::ReadModel` (ActiveRecord), `self.table_name = "<name>_read"`.
- `queries/` — read the read models.
- `dto/<...>_dto.rb` — `< Shaolin::DTO`; `json { required(:x).filled(:string) }`; `.validate(params)` → `success?/failure?/to_h/errors`.
- `controllers/<name>_controller.rb` — `< Shaolin::HTTP::Controller`; `routes do post "/x", :create; get "/x/:id", :show end`; validate DTO → `command_bus.call(Command.new(...))` → `render_result(result, location:)`; reads via read models; `command_bus`/`query_bus`/`event_store` are available. Routes may declare `response:` (OpenAPI) and `auth: :scheme` (a named authenticator registered via `HTTP.register_provider!(auth: { scheme: ->(env){identity|nil} })`; runs before the action, 401 on nil, identity in `Shaolin::Context[:identity]`; `default_auth :scheme` for a controller-wide default).
- `reactors/<entity>_reactor.rb` (optional) — `< Shaolin::Jobs::Reactor`; `on(Events::X) { |e| ... }`.
  Like a projection, but the handler runs LATER in `shaolin worker` via the outbox (NOT in the
  event's transaction). At-least-once delivery → handlers MUST be idempotent. Use for side effects:
  send email, publish to RabbitMQ (`Shaolin::Kernel["messaging.publisher"].publish(...)`), call an API.
- `db/migrate/*_create_<name>_read.rb` — read-model migration.
- `CONTRACT.md` — generated summary; read it to understand a module without its internals.

## Rules for an agent changing a module

1. Stay inside one module folder. Cross-module access only via declared `imports`/`exports` — from a
   controller, command/query handler, **reactor**, or any service object that does `include
   Shaolin::Imports`, use `import("other.thing")` (validated against the manifest; `lint` flags
   undeclared keys — it scans every `*.rb`, `reactors/` included). React to another module's events by
   topic (`on("mod.event")` + `imports events:`). Never reach into `Kernel["kernel.containers"]` by hand.
2. Reference sibling files with explicit `require_relative` (no autoloading).
3. To add a field: edit the command (attribute), event (data), DTO (rule), aggregate (apply),
   projection (write), and the read-model migration (column). Keep them consistent.
4. Domain invariants needing history live in the aggregate; shape/format checks live in the DTO.
5. Run `bundle exec rspec`. Verify HTTP with `shaolin server` + curl.

## Result → HTTP

`Success` → 200 (201 with `location:`); `Failure([:not_found,..])` → 404; `Failure([:conflict,..])`
→ 409; validation/other → 422. Bodies: `{ "error": { "code", "message" } }`.

## Async: reactors, worker, scheduler, RabbitMQ

- **Reactor** = an async event handler for side effects (event-sourced modules only). Scaffold with
  `g module <name> --es --reactor` or add `reactors/<x>_reactor.rb` by hand. When its subscribed event is appended, the `:jobs`
  provider inserts an outbox row **in the same transaction** as the event (transactional outbox —
  no lost effect on a crash between commit and side effect).
- **Reacting to ANOTHER module's event (cross-module, isolation-clean):** subscribe by the dotted
  **topic string**, not the class — `on("orders.order_placed") { |event| ... }` — and declare it in the
  manifest: `Shaolin.module("notifications") { imports events: ["orders.order_placed"] }`. A string is
  not a constant, so `lint` stays clean; the `:jobs` provider resolves the topic to its event class
  (`orders.order_placed` → `Orders::Events::OrderPlaced`) at wire time and the worker dispatches it like
  any other event. `describe --json` shows the reactor's `topics` + the module's `events_subscribed`;
  `graph` shows the `B -> A` edge. Own-module events still use the class form `on(EventClass)`.
- `shaolin worker` claims due rows (`FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED`), loads the event, runs the reactor,
  marks done; on error it retries with exponential backoff `[1s,10s,60s,600s,3600s]` and dead-letters
  after exhausting attempts. At-least-once → **reactors must be idempotent**.
- `Shaolin.schedule("name", every: "5m") { command_bus.call(...) }` (in `config/` or a module) +
  `shaolin scheduler` fires periodic work; a Postgres advisory lock guarantees a single leader across
  replicas.
- **Cross-service (RabbitMQ):** a reactor publishes an `IntegrationEvent` via
  `Shaolin::Kernel["messaging.publisher"]` (swap the in-memory publisher for
  `Shaolin::RabbitMQ::Publisher` — same `Shaolin::Messaging::Publisher` port). Another service runs
  `Shaolin::RabbitMQ::Consumer.connect(queue:, routing_keys:).run { |env| command_bus.call(...) }`.
  Publishing through the outbox makes delivery reliable.

## Redis: cache, store, broker (`shaolin-redis`)

`Shaolin::Redis.register_provider!(url:, namespace:)` registers `redis.pool`, `redis.cache`,
`cache.store`, `redis.store`, `redis.publisher` in the kernel. All on `redis-rb` + connection_pool.
- **Cache** (`Shaolin::Redis::Cache`, implements the `Shaolin::Cache` port — swap with the in-memory
  `Shaolin::Cache::Memory`): `cache.fetch("k", ttl: 300) { compute }`, `write/read/delete/clear`.
  Server-side TTL (shared across processes); JSON values with symbol keys.
- **Store** (`Shaolin::Redis::Store` — Redis as a database): `set/get` (JSON), `increment` (native),
  `hset/hget/hgetall`, `keys(pattern)`. For read models, sessions, counters, LLM state. Source of
  truth (no implicit TTL), unlike the cache.
- **Broker — Streams** (reliable): `Shaolin::Redis::StreamPublisher` implements the same
  `Shaolin::Messaging::Publisher` port as RabbitMQ (publish via `XADD`); `StreamConsumer` uses
  consumer groups (`XREADGROUP`/`XACK`, `XAUTOCLAIM` to reclaim a crashed worker's entries) for
  at-least-once consumption. A reactor can publish to Redis Streams or RabbitMQ with zero code change.
- **Broker — Pub/Sub** (`Shaolin::Redis::PubSub`): fire-and-forget fan-out (no persistence/acks).

## Production notes

- **Atomic outbox (automatic).** With the `:active_record` provider, a command's unit of work runs in
  ONE DB transaction — event append + sync projections + outbox enqueue commit or roll back together.
  No manual `transaction` needed; a crash can't leave an event without its reactor job. Enqueue is
  idempotent (unique `(reactor, event_id)`), so re-publishing never duplicates a job.
- **Connection pool.** Set `DB_POOL` >= concurrent fibers/threads. `shaolin worker --threads N` needs
  `DB_POOL>=N` (it warns otherwise). Falcon is fiber-per-request → `shaolin server` sets `:fiber`
  isolation automatically; the worker stays `:thread`.
- **Worker batching.** `WORKER_BATCH` (default 20) bounds jobs per drain. `WORKER_TX_PER_JOB=1` commits
  each job in its OWN transaction — use it for **IO-bound reactors** (outbound HTTP) so a slow call
  holds a row lock for just that one job. Default (batch in one transaction) suits fast CPU-bound work.
- **Middleware → controller channel.** Auth/middleware sets `Shaolin::Context[:project_id] = …`; the
  action reads `Shaolin::Context[:project_id]` (or the raw `request.env`). Context is fiber/thread-local,
  cleared per request, and auto-merged into logs. `Shaolin::Tenant` is the specialized tenant case.
- **Deterministic ids.** `Shaolin::Id.deterministic(*business_keys)` → a stable v5-style UUID for
  idempotent ingest (same keys → same aggregate/stream id); `Shaolin::Id.generate` for new entities.
- **Test isolation.** `Shaolin::Testing.install(rspec_config, only: :integration)` truncates read
  models + event store + outbox before each integration example (wired into the generated spec_helper);
  `Shaolin::Testing.clean!` to call directly.
- **Migrations are a release step.** With `SHAOLIN_ENV=production`, boot does NOT auto-create schema or
  migrate; run `shaolin migrate` once per release (event-store + jobs schema + read-model migrations,
  advisory-locked so concurrent replicas don't race). In dev it's automatic. **Never edit an applied
  migration** — `migrate` checksums applied files and raises on drift (the edit would never reach a DB
  that already has the version). In dev `shaolin db reset` to re-apply; in prod add a new migration.
- **HTTP limits.** Request bodies are capped (`SHAOLIN_MAX_BODY_BYTES`, default 1 MiB → 413). Every
  response carries `x-request-id` (propagated from an inbound header). **Admission control:** set
  `SHAOLIN_WEB_CONCURRENCY` ≈ `DB_POOL` to bound in-flight requests — past the cap the server load-sheds
  with 503 instead of oversubscribing the pool (off by default). `SHAOLIN_REQUEST_TIMEOUT` (seconds)
  bounds each request on Falcon (cooperative async timeout → 503), freeing a hung handler's fiber + DB
  connection.
- **Metrics.** `/metrics` (Prometheus) reports `shaolin_up`, `shaolin_db_pool{state}` (size/busy/idle/
  waiting), `shaolin_http_in_flight`/`_concurrency_max`, outbox depth by status, and
  `shaolin_outbox_oldest_pending_seconds` (worker lag) — the signals that predict pool/queue saturation.
- **Request bodies.** `Shaolin::HTTP::Request#params` parses JSON, `multipart/form-data`, and
  `application/x-www-form-urlencoded` (symbol keys); uploaded files surface via `req.files`
  (`{ field => { filename:, type:, bytes:, tempfile: } }`); `req.cookies` reads request cookies.
- **Responses.** Actions return a `Shaolin::HTTP::Response` (`json`/`text`/`created`/`no_content`/
  `render_result` build it); chain `.cookie(name, value, **opts)` / `.delete_cookie` / `.header(k,v)`,
  or `json(data, status:, headers:, cookies:)`. Raw Rack tuples still work. CORS/security headers go in
  the middleware hook, not per-action.
- **Unified logger `Shaolin::Log`** — one structured pipeline for everything (HTTP request line, worker,
  scheduler, app): `Shaolin::Log.info("msg", **fields)`, leveled (`SHAOLIN_LOG_LEVEL`), JSON in
  production / pretty in dev, fiber-local context (`Shaolin::Log.with(run_id:) { }`) + auto
  tenant/request_id correlation, pluggable sinks (`Sinks::Stdout`/`Pretty`/`Batch`).
  `SHAOLIN_LOG_EVERYTHING=1` logs every command/query/event (full firehose; the event store stays the
  durable source of truth). `SHAOLIN_LOG=off` silences. BigQuery the easy way: stdout JSON → Cloud
  Logging → a Log Router BigQuery sink (zero app code). See `docs/LOGGING.md`.
- **Error boundary.** Any exception escaping a controller becomes the JSON error contract, never a raw
  stack trace; in production the 500 message is generic. Optimistic-concurrency clashes
  (`RubyEventStore::WrongExpectedEventVersion`) map to 409 automatically.
- **Probes.** `GET /healthz` liveness (always 200), `GET /readyz` readiness (runs `Shaolin::Health`
  checks — the AR provider registers a DB ping, the Redis provider a PING; 503 if any is down),
  `GET /metrics` Prometheus text (`shaolin_up`, outbox queue depth by status).
- **Custom middleware (auth / rate limit / CORS).** It's plain Rack, so plug in the ecosystem via
  `Shaolin::HTTP.register_provider!(middleware: [->(app){ Rack::Cors.new(app){ ... } }, ->(app){ Rack::Attack.new(app) }])`
  — each builder wraps the app, runs inside the error boundary + request logger, before the router, and
  can short-circuit (e.g. 401/429). Recommended gems: **rack-cors** (CORS), **rack-attack** (rate
  limiting / throttling), **rack-session**, and for auth **Rack::Auth::Basic** (built in), **warden**,
  or a **jwt** middleware. NB: **Devise is Rails-coupled** (ActionController/Warden-via-Rails) and does
  NOT drop into a standalone Rack app — use warden/jwt directly here.
- **Async observability.** Worker and scheduler emit structured JSON logs (reactor.done/retry/dead,
  schedule.fired/failed). A failing reactor retries/dead-letters; a failing scheduled task is isolated
  (logged, never aborts the loop or the other tasks).
- **Multi-tenancy.** `Shaolin::Tenant.with(id) { ... }` / `Shaolin::Tenant.current` carry a
  fiber/thread-scoped tenant; weave it into aggregate ids, stream prefixes, and read-model columns
  (isolation is enforced by your code — the framework only carries the value).
- **Event versioning.** Register an upcasting mapper as `cqrs.event_mapper` (a RES PipelineMapper with
  `Transformation::Upcast`) — see `docs/EVENTS.md`.
- **Operating the queue.** `shaolin jobs stats` (counts by status), `shaolin jobs dead` (inspect
  dead-lettered), `shaolin jobs retry <id>` (re-queue after a fix). Migrations: `shaolin migrate`
  (release step), `shaolin rollback [STEPS]`.
- **DB pool resilience.** `DB_POOL`, `DB_CHECKOUT_TIMEOUT`, `DB_REAPING_FREQUENCY` tune the pool
  (reaping reclaims connections dropped by a failover).

## LLM harnesses (`shaolin-llm` + `shaolin-harness`)

Build LLM harnesses as **event-sourced gate state machines** — durable, auditable, replayable.
- `shaolin-llm`: `Shaolin::LLM::Client#complete(messages:, tools:)` → `Completion(text, reasoning,
  tool_calls, usage)`; adapters `InMemory` (scripted/deterministic, for tests) and `OpenAI` (key from
  `ENV["OPENAI_API_KEY"]`; `open_timeout:`/`read_timeout:` configurable, read default 600s so slow
  reasoning replies don't hit Net::HTTP's 60s default; a non-2xx response raises a typed
  `Shaolin::LLM::HTTPError` (no JSON-parse of an HTML gateway page), with built-in retry of transient
  5xx/timeouts via `max_retries:`/`retry_backoff:`; sampling params via `complete(params:)` /
  `OpenAI.new(default_params:)` so a reasoning reply isn't truncated by the server default max_tokens).
  `Completion` carries `text`/`reasoning`/`tool_calls`/`usage`/`data`/`finish_reason` (`truncated?` on
  `"length"`); `OpenAI.new(max_concurrency: N)` bounds in-flight calls (semaphore) against a
  capacity-limited provider. **Audio:** `client.speak(text, voice:, format:)` (TTS→bytes) +
  `client.transcribe(audio_bytes, language:)` (STT→text), same reliability layer; async TTS via
  `OpenAI.new(tts_async: {...})`; `InMemory` scripts `speak:`/`transcribe:`. `:llm` provider →
  `llm.client`. Realtime streaming is a separate substrate.
  **Reasoning:** `Completion#reasoning` carries the model's thinking trace — mapped from a
  `reasoning_content`/`reasoning` field automatically, or lifted out of an inline `<think>…</think>`
  block when you opt in with `OpenAI.new(reasoning_tag: "think")` (off by default; for Qwen-style
  models). `text` stays clean; the harness persists `reasoning` in its `Responded` event.
  **Structured output:** pass `response_format:` (`{type:"json_schema",json_schema:{…}}` / `json_object`)
  to `complete` → parsed object on `Completion#data` (symbol keys; nil unless requested). For
  classification/decision gates, cleaner than a pseudo-tool — a gate declares `response_format { … }` and
  `on_result` reads `out.data`.
- `shaolin-harness`: subclass `Shaolin::Harness`; declare `gate :name, entry:/terminal: do prompt {…};
  tools name: CommandClass; on_result { |out, run| run.transition_to(:next) / run.complete(...) } end`.
  **Tools are commands on the command bus.** Each step (prompt/response/tool/transition) is a domain
  event on the run's stream. Drive with `Shaolin::Harness::Runner`: `run_to_completion(input:)` (sync)
  or `start` + `advance(id)` per gate (durable; a fresh Runner resumes from the event stream — IO is
  outside the per-step transaction, at-least-once → keep tools idempotent). For a **worker-driven**
  loop, `Shaolin::Harness.register_durable_provider!` + run `shaolin worker` (`WORKER_TX_PER_JOB=1`):
  each `GateEntered` enqueues a DriveReactor advance, which enqueues the next gate — the run advances
  across worker ticks, crash-resumable. Gates take an optional `to: [:a, :b]` (declared next gates for
  visualization). Harnesses live in `app/harnesses/**`; `shaolin describe --json` lists them
  (gates/tools/model/edges) and `shaolin graph` draws the gate graph. Test with `Shaolin::LLM::InMemory`
  for deterministic replay (no network).
- **Two modes, one engine.** A harness is a state machine over LLM steps. *Autonomous* (above): input
  fixed at start, runs to a terminal gate. *Conversational* (`Shaolin::Conversation < Shaolin::Harness`):
  a human message per turn, rests at an `await` gate between turns, never terminal — for chatbots/
  companions. Declare `stages`/`edges` (a STRICT, queryable funnel — `run.advance_to` rejects undeclared
  jumps), `context`/`window` (persona + recent-window memory the prompt builder reads), `gate :x, await:
  true` (resting state), and optional `on_turn`. Drive with `MyConvo.session(id:, llm:, repo:,
  command_bus:).receive(message)` → reply; state updates ride tools=commands + on_result/on_turn (no
  reply-parsing). A turn is itself an autonomous mini-run that ends by resting at the await gate, so both
  modes compose in one event-sourced run. **Canned gates** reply with fixed text and make NO LLM call —
  `gate :refuse, reply: "…"` (or `reply { |run| … }`) — for refusals/nudges/scripted lines (zero tokens).
  Example: `examples/conversation`. **Cross-user read-side:** opt-in
  `Shaolin::Conversation.register_read_model!` maintains a `conversations_read` projection
  (`session_id, harness, stage, turn_count, last_turn_at, tags jsonb`) so other modules query the whole
  base via the Kernel-registered `conversations.read` facade — `find(id)`, `in_stage("offer")`,
  `with_min_turns(n, since:)`, `query(stage:, tags: {geo: "DE"})` — without driving the session (CQRS
  read model; the run stream is the write side). Stamp app dims with `session.tag(geo:, variant:)` /
  `run.tag(...)` / a `tags { |run| {...} }` block; metrics stay app-side. A turn's message may be a
  String, an OpenAI content Array, or `{ text:, images: [...] }` — multimodal (image) input flows
  through `receive`, the event log, and the adapter unchanged. Gates may set `params(max_tokens: 4096)`.
- **Realtime/audio (provider-agnostic)**: `Shaolin::LLM::Realtime` — normalized session events
  (`session_started`/`transcript_delta`/`audio_delta`/`tool_call`/`turn_completed`/...), `Audio` (PCM16/
  base64/framing), a `Session`/`Client` port (`send_audio`/`send_text`/`commit`/`tool_result`/`close` +
  `on_event`). `InMemory` adapter builds/tests voice+tool flows with no provider; `OpenAI` adapter maps
  the Realtime WebSocket both ways (inject a WS transport for live). Same substrate for any provider.
  Example: `examples/realtime`.

## Reference

- Working example: `examples/demo` (a `users` module). Run `ruby examples/demo/verify.rb`.
- LLM harness example: `examples/harness` — `ruby examples/harness/verify.rb` (gates + tool=command,
  sync + durable resume, on the InMemory stub; no network/key).
- Reactor/outbox example: `examples/reactor` — `ruby examples/reactor/verify.rb` (publish event →
  outbox row → `worker.run_once` → reactor side effect).
- Redis example: `examples/redis` — `ruby examples/redis/verify.rb` (cache + store + Streams broker;
  needs a local `redis-server`).
- Cross-module reactor example: `examples/cross_module` — `ruby examples/cross_module/verify.rb`
  (module B reacts to module A's event by topic; isolation-clean).
- What changed recently / upgrading: `CHANGELOG.md`.
- Logging & shipping logs to BigQuery: `docs/LOGGING.md`.
- Design specs: `docs/superpowers/specs/`.
- Stack: dry-system, ruby_event_store + aggregate_root, ActiveRecord (Postgres), dry-validation/
  dry-struct, hanami-router, Falcon/Puma, Thor; jobs/outbox (PG `SKIP LOCKED` + advisory locks),
  messaging + RabbitMQ transport (bunny — pure Ruby); Redis cache/store/broker (redis-rb + Streams).
